titel: The requirement for food service has been removed for serving permits.
html:
The proposal suggests simpler rules for serving licenses and the sale of beer. The proposals include the abolition of the following requirements and regulations:

  • The requirement for food service and a separate kitchen for serving licenses.
  • The regulations regarding seating and bars.
  • The requirement for a separate kitchen for serving licenses for catering businesses.
  • The requirement for food service when selling beer.

One or more government agencies should be tasked with monitoring and evaluating the abolition of the requirement for food service and a separate kitchen.

The proposed amendments to the law should come into effect on June 1, 2026.

Proposed Resolution of the Parliament

Prop. 2025/26:221

Government Proposal: The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Alcohol Act (2010:1622). 3

Prop. 2025/26:221 2 Draft law amending the Alcohol Act (2010:1622)

Hereby, with regard to the Alcohol Act (2010:1622), it is stipulated that:

  1. Section 8, paragraph 15, shall cease to be in effect;
  2. Sections 8, paragraphs 4 and 8, shall have the following wording;
  3. The heading immediately preceding section 8, paragraph 14, shall be "Premises".

Current wording Proposed wording

Section 8, Paragraph 4 A person who operates a catering business for private groups may be granted a permanent serving license on the condition that the premises where the service takes place is registered with and approved by the municipality for each occasion. A person who operates a catering business must have their own kitchen for preparing food in order to obtain a permanent serving license.

Paragraph 8 The sale of beer may, subject to the restrictions prescribed in this Act, take place if the business is operated in premises or other spaces in buildings or means of transport that:

  1. are part of facilities approved according to regulations issued pursuant to section 7, paragraph 1 of the Food Act (2006:804) or registered according to the European Parliament and Council Regulation (EC) No 852/2004 of April 29, 2004, on food hygiene, and
  2. are intended for permanent activities involving food and where food is served simultaneously.
  1. are intended for permanent activities involving food.

Despite the first paragraph, beer may be sold in cases as specified in sections 1a and 1b, or by a person who holds a serving license. A person who intends to operate a business selling beer shall register the business with the municipality where the sale will take place. The sale may not begin before the registration has been made. The foregoing does not apply in cases as specified in sections 1a and 1b, or to a person who holds a serving license. A person who is required to register under the third paragraph shall exercise special control (self-monitoring) over the sale. There shall be a program suitable for the business for the self-monitoring.

  1. This Act shall enter into force on June 1, 2026.

1 Latest wording of section 8, paragraph 15, 2012:205.
tldr: The government proposes making it easier to obtain permits to serve alcohol. They want to remove the requirement that food must be served in order to sell beer and cocktails.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a plan to simplify the regulations for alcohol serving licenses. Currently, businesses selling alcohol must also serve food and have their own kitchen. This proposal aims to eliminate those requirements.

For catering businesses, which deliver food and beverages to events, the requirement for a separate kitchen will be removed. It will also become easier to serve beer, as the requirement to serve food simultaneously with the beer will be lifted. Regulations regarding seating and bars will also be changed.

The proposal also aims to simplify the regulations for premises. The authorities will monitor how these changes function after they are implemented. The goal is to make it less complicated for businesses to operate with alcohol.

If the parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the new regulations will come into effect on June 1, 2026. This means that businesses planning their operations in advance need to be aware of this date.
